infobox.value: **[Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan "Afghanistan")** – Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan | A UN member state | A None | The ruling Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan, in power since 2021, [has not been recognised](/wiki/Recognition_of_the_Islamic_Emirate_of_Afghanistan "Recognition of the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan") by the United Nations or any other state except Russia.[[5]](#cite_note-11) The defunct  [Islamic Republic of Afghanistan](/wiki/Islamic_Republic_of_Afghanistan "Islamic Republic of Afghanistan") remains the recognised government.[[6]](#cite_note-akhund2-12)[[7]](#cite_note-UN_Seats_Denied-13)

A UN member state | A None | Antigua and Barbuda is a [Commonwealth realm](/wiki/Commonwealth_realm "Commonwealth realm")[[g]](#cite_note-realm-15) with one autonomous region, [Barbuda](/wiki/Barbuda "Barbuda").[[9]](#cite_note-16)[[h]](#cite_note-autonomous-17)

– Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan | A UN member state | A None | The ruling Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan, in power since 2021, [has not been recognised](/wiki/Recognition_of_the_Islamic_Emirate_of_Afghanistan "Recognition of the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan") by the United Nations or any other state except Russia.[[5]](#cite_note-11) The defunct  [Islamic Republic of Afghanistan](/wiki/Islamic_Republic_of_Afghanistan "Islamic Republic of Afghanistan") remains the recognised government.[[6]](#cite_note-akhund2-12)[[7]](#cite_note-UN_Seats_Denied-13) |
– Republic of Albania | A UN member state | A None |  |
– People's Democratic Republic of Algeria | A UN member state | A None |  |

– Principality of Andorra | A UN member state | A None | Andorra is a [co-principality](/wiki/Coregency#Andorra "Coregency") in which the office of head of state is jointly held *ex officio* by the [French president](/wiki/President_of_France "President of France") and the bishop of the Roman Catholic [diocese of Urgell](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Urgell "Roman Catholic Diocese of Urgell"),[[8]](#cite_note-14) who himself is appointed with approval from the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See"). |
– Republic of Angola | A UN member state | A None |  |

– Argentine Republic[[i]](#cite_note-18) | A UN member state | A None | Argentina is a [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation") of [23 provinces and one autonomous city](/wiki/Provinces_of_Argentina "Provinces of Argentina").[[j]](#cite_note-federal-19)[[k]](#cite_note-ArgentineAntarctica-21) |
– Republic of Armenia | A UN member state | A None |  |

– Commonwealth of Australia | A UN member state | A None | Australia is a [Commonwealth realm](/wiki/Commonwealth_realm "Commonwealth realm")[[g]](#cite_note-realm-15) and a [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation") of both [states and territories](/wiki/States_and_territories_of_Australia "States and territories of Australia"). There are six states, three internal territories, six external territories and one [claimed](/wiki/Territorial_claims_in_Antarctica "Territorial claims in Antarctica") Antarctic external territory. The external territories of Australia are:  * [Ashmore and Cartier Islands](/wiki/Ashmore_and_Cartier_Islands "Ashmore and Cartier Islands") * [Christmas Island](/wiki/Christmas_Island "Christmas Island") * [Cocos (Keeling) Islands](/wiki/Cocos_(Keeling)_Islands "Cocos (Keeling) Islands") * [Coral Sea Islands Territory](/wiki/Coral_Sea_Islands_Territory "Coral Sea Islands Territory") * [Heard Island and McDonald Islands](/wiki/Heard_Island_and_McDonald_Islands "Heard Island and McDonald Islands") * [Norfolk Island](/wiki/Norfolk_Island "Norfolk Island") * *[Australian Antarctic Territory](/wiki/Australian_Antarctic_Territory "Australian Antarctic Territory")* |

– Republic of Austria | A UN member state | A None | Member of the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union").[[e]](#cite_note-EU-9) Austria is a [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation") of [nine states](/wiki/Federal_states_of_Austria "Federal states of Austria").[[j]](#cite_note-federal-19) |

[![A long row of flags](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Palais_des_Nations_unies%2C_%C3%A0_Gen%C3%A8ve.jpg/330px-Palais_des_Nations_unies%2C_%C3%A0_Gen%C3%A8ve.jpg)](/wiki/File:Palais_des_Nations_unies,_%C3%A0_Gen%C3%A8ve.jpg)

Flags of the [United Nations member](/wiki/Member_states_of_the_United_Nations "Member states of the United Nations") and non-member GA observer states in front of the [Palace of Nations](/wiki/Palace_of_Nations "Palace of Nations") in [Geneva](/wiki/Geneva "Geneva"), Switzerland

The following is a list providing an overview of [sovereign states](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state") around the world with information on their status and recognition of their [sovereignty](/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty").

The 205 listed states can be divided into three categories based on membership within the [United Nations System](/wiki/United_Nations_System "United Nations System"): 193 [UN member states](/wiki/Member_states_of_the_United_Nations "Member states of the United Nations"),[[1]](#cite_note-unms-1) 2 [UN General Assembly non-member observer states](/wiki/United_Nations_General_Assembly_observers#Current_non-member_observers "United Nations General Assembly observers"), and 10 other states. The *sovereignty dispute* column indicates states having undisputed sovereignty (189 states, of which there are 188 UN member states and one UN General Assembly non-member observer state), states having disputed sovereignty (14 states, of which there are 5 UN member states, 1 UN General Assembly non-member observer state, and 8 de facto states), and states having a [special political status](/wiki/Political_status_of_the_Cook_Islands_and_Niue "Political status of the Cook Islands and Niue") (two states, both in [free association](/wiki/Associated_state "Associated state") with New Zealand).

Compiling a list such as this can be complicated and controversial, as there is no definition that is binding on all the members of the community of nations concerning the criteria for [statehood](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)"). For more information on the criteria used to determine the contents of this list, please see the *[criteria for inclusion](#Criteria_for_inclusion)* section below. The list is intended to include entities that have been recognised as having *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* status as sovereign states, and inclusion should not be seen as an endorsement of any specific claim to statehood in legal terms.

## Criteria for inclusion

The dominant [customary international law](/wiki/Customary_international_law "Customary international law") standard of statehood is the [declarative theory of statehood](/wiki/Sovereign_state#Declarative_theory "Sovereign state"), which was codified by the [Montevideo Convention](/wiki/Montevideo_Convention "Montevideo Convention") of 1933. The Convention defines the state as a [person](/wiki/Legal_person "Legal person") of international law if it "possess[es] the following qualifications: (a) a permanent population; (b) a defined territory; (c) government; and (d) a capacity to enter into relations with the other states" so long as it was not "obtained by force whether this consists in the employment of arms, in threatening diplomatic representations, or in any other effective coercive measure".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Debate exists on the degree to which [recognition](/wiki/Diplomatic_recognition "Diplomatic recognition") should be included as a criterion of statehood. The declarative theory of statehood argues that statehood is purely objective and recognition of a state by other states is irrelevant. On the other end of the spectrum, the [constitutive theory of statehood](/wiki/Sovereign_state#Constitutive_theory "Sovereign state") defines a state as a person under international law only if it is recognised as [sovereign](/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty") by other states. For the purposes of this list, included are all polities that consider themselves sovereign states (through a [declaration of independence](/wiki/Declaration_of_independence "Declaration of independence") or some other means) and either:

* are often regarded as satisfying the declarative theory of statehood, *or*
* are recognised as a sovereign state by at least one [UN member state](/wiki/Member_states_of_the_United_Nations "Member states of the United Nations")

In some cases, there is a divergence of opinion over the interpretation of the first point, and whether an entity satisfies it is disputed. Unique political entities which fail to meet the classification of a sovereign state are considered [proto-states](/wiki/Quasi-state "Quasi-state").[[3]](#cite_note-Hahn-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Griffiths-4)

On the basis of the above criteria, this list includes the following 205 entities:[[a]](#cite_note-5)[[b]](#cite_note-micros-6)

* 204 states recognised by at least one UN member state
* One state ([Transnistria](/wiki/Transnistria "Transnistria")) that satisfies the declarative theory of statehood and is recognised only by non-UN member states

The table includes bullets in the right-hand column representing entities that are either not sovereign states or have a close association to another sovereign state. It also includes subnational areas where the sovereignty of the titular state is limited by an international agreement. Taken together, these include:

* Entities that are in a [free association](/wiki/Associated_state "Associated state") relationship with another state
* Two entities controlled by Pakistan which are neither sovereign states, dependent territories, nor part of another state: [Azad Kashmir](/wiki/Azad_Kashmir "Azad Kashmir") and [Gilgit-Baltistan](/wiki/Gilgit-Baltistan "Gilgit-Baltistan")
* Dependent territories of another state, as well as areas that exhibit many characteristics of dependent territories according to the [dependent territory](/wiki/Dependent_territory "Dependent territory") page
* Subnational entities created by international agreements

The ROC was a founding member of the UN and enjoyed membership from 1945 to 1971, with veto power in the [UN Security Council](/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council "United Nations Security Council"). 17. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-TAI2_32-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-TAI2_32-1) In 1949, the Republic of China government led by the [Kuomintang](/wiki/Kuomintang "Kuomintang") (KMT) lost the [Chinese Civil War](/wiki/Chinese_Civil_War "Chinese Civil War") to the [Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party "Chinese Communist Party") (CCP) and set up a provisional capital in [Taipei](/wiki/Taipei "Taipei"). The CCP established the PRC. As such, the [political status of the ROC and legal status of Taiwan](/wiki/Political_status_of_Taiwan "Political status of Taiwan") (alongside the [territories](/wiki/Taiwan_Area "Taiwan Area") under ROC jurisdiction) are in dispute. In 1971, the United Nations gave the [China seat](/wiki/China_and_the_United_Nations "China and the United Nations") to the PRC. In the view of the United Nations, no member of the organization withdrew as a consequence of this, but the ROC representatives declared that they were withdrawing. Most states recognise the PRC to be the [sole legitimate representative](/wiki/One_China "One China") of all China, and the UN classifies Taiwan as "[Taiwan, Province of China](/wiki/Taiwan_Province,_People%27s_Republic_of_China "Taiwan Province, People's Republic of China")". The ROC has de facto relations with most sovereign states. A significant political movement within Taiwan advocates [Taiwan independence](/wiki/Taiwan_independence_movement "Taiwan independence movement").

See [Name of the Czech Republic](/wiki/Name_of_the_Czech_Republic "Name of the Czech Republic").
22. **[^](#cite_ref-38)** The designation "Denmark" can refer either to Metropolitan Denmark or to the entire Danish Realm (e.g. in international organisations).
23. **[^](#cite_ref-41)** Formerly referred to as the Kingdom of Swaziland, its official name until 2018.
24. **[^](#cite_ref-44)** [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland") was demilitarized by the [Treaty of Paris](/wiki/Treaty_of_Paris_(1856) "Treaty of Paris (1856)") in 1856, which was later affirmed by the [League of Nations](/wiki/League_of_Nations "League of Nations") in 1921, and in a somewhat different context reaffirmed in the treaty on [Finland's admission to the European Union in 1995](/wiki/1995_enlargement_of_the_European_Union "1995 enlargement of the European Union").
25. **[^](#cite_ref-Adélie_45-0)** France's [claimed](/wiki/Territorial_claims_in_Antarctica "Territorial claims in Antarctica") Antarctic territory of *[Adélie Land](/wiki/Ad%C3%A9lie_Land "Adélie Land")* (*Terre Adélie*) is one of five constituent districts of the French Southern and Antarctic Lands.
26. **[^](#cite_ref-48)** Also known as Guinea-Conakry.
27. **[^](#cite_ref-Republic_52-0)** While sometimes referred to as the "Republic of Iceland"[[23]](#cite_note-49)[[24]](#cite_note-50) and sometimes its counterpart *Lýðveldið Ísland* in Icelandic, the official name of the country is simply "Iceland".[[25]](#cite_note-51) One example of the former is the name of the [Constitution of Iceland](/wiki/Constitution_of_Iceland "Constitution of Iceland"), which in Icelandic is *Stjórnarskrá lýðveldisins Íslands* and is translated to "the Constitution of the republic of Iceland". However, in this usage, "republic" is not capitalized.
28. **[^](#cite_ref-55)** "Ireland" is the official name of the country in English. "Republic of Ireland" (the official *description* in English) and "Éire" (the official name in Irish) have sometimes been used unofficially to distinguish the state from the larger [island of Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"), however, this is officially deprecated.[[27]](#cite_note-54) See [names of the Irish state](/wiki/Names_of_the_Irish_state "Names of the Irish state").
29. **[^](#cite_ref-62)** The government of Ivory Coast uses "Côte d'Ivoire" as the official English name of the country.
30. **[^](#cite_ref-63)** The country's official name of Myanmar, adopted in 1989, has been mixed and controversial, with the former name Burma still being used in many cases. See [Names of Myanmar](/wiki/Names_of_Myanmar "Names of Myanmar").
31. **[^](#cite_ref-64)** The designation "the Netherlands" can refer either to the Metropolitan [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") or to the entire [Kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_of_the_Netherlands "Kingdom of the Netherlands") (e.g. in international organisations).
32. **[^](#cite_ref-69)** Formerly known constitutionally as the "Republic of Macedonia" from 1991 to 2019 and under the international designation of "the former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia" (FYROM) from 1993 to 2019 due to the [Macedonia naming dispute](/wiki/Macedonia_naming_dispute "Macedonia naming dispute") with [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"). Following the [Prespa Agreement](/wiki/Prespa_Agreement "Prespa Agreement") going into effect in February 2019, the country was renamed "North Macedonia".
33. **[^](#cite_ref-Plazas_87-0)** Spain holds several small overseas territories scattered along the Mediterranean coast bordering [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco"), known as the [plazas de soberanía](/wiki/Plazas_de_soberan%C3%ADa "Plazas de soberanía").
34. **[^](#cite_ref-88)** Formerly known as [Ceylon](/wiki/Dominion_of_Ceylon "Dominion of Ceylon") until 1972.
35. **[^](#cite_ref-89)** Formerly also known as East Timor.
36. **[^](#cite_ref-90)** Formerly the "Republic of Turkey". In 2023, the United Nations recognized "Türkiye" as the official English name of the country after a request made by the Turkish government.
37. **[^](#cite_ref-91)** There is no official flag for Akrotiri and Dhekelia; it is represented by the [flag of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom "Flag of the United Kingdom"), the [Union Jack](/wiki/Union_Jack "Union Jack"). See [Flag of Akrotiri and Dhekelia](/wiki/Flag_of_Akrotiri_and_Dhekelia "Flag of Akrotiri and Dhekelia")
38. **[^](#cite_ref-92)** There is no official flag for Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha; it is represented by the [flag of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom "Flag of the United Kingdom"), the [Union Jack](/wiki/Union_Jack "Union Jack"). However, the 3 islands that comprise it do have flags. See [Flag of Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha](/wiki/Flag_of_Saint_Helena,_Ascension_and_Tristan_da_Cunha "Flag of Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha")
